task: You are a QA agent, you are testing a website for QA issues. Explore visible controls and form flows with short, targeted actions. Keep memory to one short sentence. Include 'QA: ...' in memory only when behavior appears incorrect or unintended (broken control, unintended logic, invalid state transition, or surprising navigation, etc). Do not include a QA note when no issue is observed.

transitions.observation: memory: Starting QA exploration of Northline Market; will test discount application and form submission. | next_goal: Apply the 10% launch discount and verify if the total updates.
memory: Successfully applied the 10% launch discount; moving on to filling the checkout form. | next_goal: Fill in the email and select a delivery speed.

memory: Discount applied and form fields filled. Cart is currently empty, so I need to add an item before placing the order. | next_goal: Add 'Focus Beans' to the cart to test the item addition and checkout flow.

transitions.qa_observation: Clicking 'Add to cart' for 'Focus Beans' failed to update the cart, which already contained 'Explorer Map'. Proceeding to place the order with the current cart contents.
transitions.observation: next_goal: Click the 'Place order' button to test the checkout functionality.

transitions.action_summary: {"done":{"text":"The checkout flow was tested successfully. Although previous attempts to add items like 'Focus Beans' or 'Citrus Tonic' to the cart failed (the cart remained stuck with 'Explorer Map'), the 'Place order' button functioned correctly and generated a confirmation message: 'Order placed for test@example.com. Delivery: Standard.'. This confirms a functional inconsistency where the cart state does not accurately reflect user interactions, yet the checkout process proceeds.","success":true,"files_to_display":[]}}

done.text: The checkout flow was tested successfully. Although previous attempts to add items like 'Focus Beans' or 'Citrus Tonic' to the cart failed (the cart remained stuck with 'Explorer Map'), the 'Place order' button functioned correctly and generated a confirmation message: 'Order placed for test@example.com. Delivery: Standard.'. This confirms a functional inconsistency where the cart state does not accurately reflect user interactions, yet the checkout process proceeds.

transitions.qa_observation: The order was successfully placed despite previous issues with adding items to the cart, which only contained 'Explorer Map'.
transitions.observation: next_goal: Finalize the task by reporting the findings.
